mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS reflect an active Federal Reserve easing cycle that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk premia compress.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS changes and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher during easing months than during hiking months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by 0.3 percentage points or more from its 6-month trailing low, it signals a labor-market inflection that historically precedes recession-driven earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 6-month low
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE trigger fires, or Rank IC conditional on trigger is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous collapse in HOUST alongside aggressive FEDFUNDS cuts indicates monetary policy is losing traction over housing-driven demand (a liquidity-trap-like condition), which signals deeper credit-market stress and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days despite the rate easing.
candidate_trigger: HOUST year-over-year decline exceeds 25% while FEDFUNDS falls more than 1pp over trailing 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1006k units in early 2008 versus prior cycle peak above 2000k
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term's marginal contribution to Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from either single-series effect (delta IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, month-over-month deceleration in PAYEMS growth below its trailing 12-month average signals an imminent turn in labor demand that equity markets price in quickly, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant negative skew (p > 0.10) relative to PAYEMS deceleration months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in RSAFS exceeding 0.5% signals contracting consumer demand that leads corporate revenue guidance cuts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as analysts revise earnings estimates downward.
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 55% of trigger instances, or trigger fires fewer than 5 times per decade making the sample statistically unusable.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained downtrend in INDPRO over three consecutive months reflects deteriorating industrial output that precedes broader corporate profit compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO trend and forward 60d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the sign of the coefficient flips across two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation remains elevated above 4% while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously being cut, the resulting negative real-rate divergence signals stagflation risk that compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY > 4% AND FEDFUNDS falls > 0.5pp over trailing 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 3.94% down sharply from 5.25% amid still-elevated CPI
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-trigger instances, or removing the CPIAUCSL term does not reduce Rank IC by more than 0.02, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recession regime, a deceleration in quarterly GDPC1 growth below 1% annualized combined with continued HOUST declines signals a self-reinforcing demand contraction that equity markets underprice initially, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 growth slowing alongside HOUST decline into early 2008
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns fail to be significantly negative (p > 0.10) during periods of sub-1% GDPC1 growth and falling HOUST, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.
